title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertions on What is the Waiver of Premium Rider for Term Insurance? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: waiver of premium, waiver of premium rider
Meta-description: Learn everything you need to know about the Waiver of Premium Rider for Term Insurance.
Body Content -
Term insurance comes with many different add-ons or ‘riders’ that can make your policy a lot more tailored to your needs. From receiving an accidental death benefit to getting more comprehensive coverage with a critical illness rider, you have a slew of additions that can fortify your insurance plan. One of these is the Waiver of Premium Rider. This rider is a very unique rider as it waives off all the future payable premiums in the situation where the life assured becomes physically disabled. Here’s everything you need to know about this insurance rider.
Waiver of Premium Rider
Essentially, with the Waiver of premium rider, you can ensure that your term policy will continue to protect you even in the situation where you are unable to make premium payments as a result of a physical disability. Suppose an accident were to result in the life assured suffering total and permanent disability. In this situation, the financial stability of their family can be ensured as their policy will continue to protect them thanks to the Waiver of premium rider, even if the life assured is unable to pay their premiums thereon.
Eligibility Criteria of Waiver of Premium Rider
To be able to add the Waiver of Premium Rider to your term insurance, the minimum age of entry is 18 years and the maximum is 65 years. The maximum maturity age for adding this rider to one’s insurance policy is 70 years.
The minimum Sum Assured is the same as the base policy’s sum assured, and there is no maximum limit as this will depend on the insured’s policy.
The coverage period of this waiver lasts as long as the life insurance policy.
There will be a waiting period of 90 days starting on the date of purchase of this waiver as an addition to your policy.
The premium term payment will remain the same as that of your life insurance policy’s term, with the payment mode for your premiums also staying the same.
Documents Required for Waiver of Premium Rider Claims
To successfully claim a waiver of premium rider for your term insurance policy, the documents mentioned below are required:
The original insurance policy document.
A claim form that has been filled out by you.
The insurance policyholder’s photo ID like their Aadhaar or PAN Card as a proof of identity.
An electricity bill or driver’s licence as the policyholder’s proof of address.
Any relevant medical reports in case the policyholder might have a critical illness.
A copy of the bank statement of the policyholder as well as a cancelled cheque.
A certificate of disability from any local governmental body.
Waiver of Premium Rider Exclusions
Before getting any rider to your term insurance policy, being aware of its exclusions is important. Here are the exclusions for the Waiver of premium rider.
Self-inflicted injuries or suicide are excluded
In case of any congenital infection, you cannot use this Waiver
Disability as a result of alcohol or substance abuse is excluded.
Having a pre-existing medical condition that develops into a disability or critical illness is excluded. P
Any form of radioactive contamination as a result of a nuclear accident
Civil commotion, war, a breach of law, or any form of willful participation in violent acts that resulted in a disability will be excluded.
Importance of Adding Waiver of Premium Rider
Policy continuance: In case of the policyholder is unable to pay their premiums, this waiver ensures that the policy will continue to protect their loved ones. This means if the policyholder passes away, is diagnosed with a critical illness during the policy tenure, or suffers permanent or total disability from an accident, this waiver will keep their loved ones financially secure, by letting the nominee avail the benefits of the policy. This way this waiver can offer protection against life’s uncertainties.
Tax benefits: The insured can continue to get tax benefits from their term insurance policy by adding the waiver of premium rider, as the premiums paid toward this rider are tax exempt as per Section 80 C of the Income Tax Act.
Affordable: This rider can be added to one’s policy at the time one issues it with an exchange of an additional premium which enhances the coverage of the policy. The premium for this waiver is nominal and the coverage of the base policy can be substantially increased at an affordable cost.
Conclusion
An added layer of security never hurts anybody. By using the Waiver of premium rider, you can enhance the coverage offered by your insurance to your loved ones in the situation where you become unable to pay your premiums. At an affordable rate, you get to continue financially protecting your loved ones against unforeseen events.
Disclaimer:
